'''''UFC 327: Procházka vs. Ulberg''''' was a [[mixed martial arts]] event produced by the [[Ultimate Fighting Championship]] that took place on April 11, 2026 at the [[Kaseya Center]] in [[Miami, Florida]], United States.<ref name="Miami">{{Cite web|url=https://www.si.com/fannation/mma/news/knockout-artists-walker-reyes-fight-miami-ufc-327|title=Devastating UFC knockout artists reportedly set to fight in Miami at UFC 327|publisher=si.com|author=Drew Beaupre|date=2025-12-31|access-date=2026-01-05}}</ref>

==Background==
The event marked the promotion's fifth visit to Miami and first since [[UFC 314]] in April 2025.<ref name="Miami"/>

A [[UFC Light Heavyweight Championship]] bout for the vacant title between former champion [[Jiří Procházka]] (also the inaugural [[Rizin Fighting Federation#Light Heavyweight Championship|Rizin Light Heavyweight Champion]]) and [[Carlos Ulberg]] headlined the event, as current two-time champion [[Alex Pereira]] (also former [[List of UFC champions#Middleweight Championship|UFC Middleweight Champion]], former [[Glory (kickboxing)#Glory Middleweight Championship|Glory Middleweight]] and [[Glory (kickboxing)#Glory Light Heavyweight Championship|Light Heavyweight Champion]]) vacated the title in order to challenge for the [[Interim championship|interim]] [[UFC Heavyweight Championship]].<ref name="Jiri">{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/story/sports/ufc/2026/02/27/ufc-327-jiri-prochazka-vs-carlos-ulberg-set-for-title-headliner/88767492007/|title=Jiri Prochazka vs. Carlos Ulberg title bout set for UFC 327 headliner|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Danny Segura|date=2025-02-27|access-date=2025-02-27}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/story/sports/ufc/2026/03/07/alex-pereira-ciryl-gane-interim-title-fight-set-for-ufc-white-house/89047309007/|title=Alex Pereira-Ciryl Gane interim title fight set for UFC White House|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Mike Bohn|date=2026-03-07|access-date=2026-03-08}}</ref> 

A [[UFC Flyweight Championship]] bout between current champion [[Joshua Van]] and [[Tatsuro Taira]] was scheduled to take place at the event.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.telecomasia.net/news/mma/joshua-van-to-defend-ufc-title-against-taira-on-april-12-in-miami/|title=Joshua Van to Defend UFC Title Against Taira on April 12 in Miami|publisher=telecomasia.net|author=Marina Magomedova|date=2026-02-13|access-date=2026-02-13}}</ref> However, one week before the event, Van withdrew due to a knee injury, so the bout was moved to [[UFC 328]], which is scheduled to take place one month later.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/ufc/480397/joshua-van-out-of-ufc-327-title-fight-with-tatsuro-taira|title=Joshua Van out of UFC 327 title fight with Tatsuro Taira|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Guilherme Cruz|date=2026-04-04|access-date=2026-04-04}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://heavy.com/sports/ufc/joshua-van-reveals-injury-fight-postponement/|title=Joshua Van reveals injury that caused UFC fight postponement|publisher=heavy.com|author=Adam Martin|date=2026-05-10|access-date=2026-05-11}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/story/sports/ufc/2026/04/04/joshua-van-vs-tatsuro-taira-title-fight-off-ufc-327-moved-ufc-328/89469964007/|title=Joshua Van vs. Tatsuro Taira title fight off UFC 327, moved to UFC 328|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Mike Bohn|date=2026-04-04|access-date=2026-04-05}}</ref> The pairing was briefly linked to [[UFC 302]] in June 2024 before being shifted to [[UFC on ESPN: Perez vs. Taira]] two weeks later, where Taira faced a different opponent and Van's bout was ultimately cancelled.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2024/04/ufc-302-tatsuro-taira-vs-joshua-van-fight-card|title=Tatsuro Taira fills UFC 302 opening, fights Joshua Van|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Nolan King|date=2024-04-25|access-date=2024-04-25}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|author=Drake Riggs|date=2024-05-13|title=Shake-up! UFC Vegas 93 re-arranges matchups, Alex Perez vs. Tatsuro Taira now headlines|url=https://www.mmamania.com/2024/5/13/24155839/shake-up-ufc-vegas-93-re-arranges-matchups-alex-perez-vs-tatsuro-taira-now-headlines-mma|access-date=2024-05-13|publisher=mmamania.com}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|date=2024-06-14|title=UFC on ESPN 58 weigh-in results: Ranked flyweight misses mark by 3.5 pounds, fight scrapped|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2024/06/ufc-espn-58-las-vegas-alex-perez-vs-tatsuro-taira-weigh-in-results-live-stream|access-date=2024-06-14|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Staff}}</ref>

A flyweight bout between [[Road to UFC Season 3#Flyweight|Road to UFC Season 3]] flyweight winner Choi Dong-hun and undefeated prospect [[André Lima (fighter)|André Lima]] was scheduled for this event.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pwinsiderxtra.com/ViewArticle.php?id=39764&p=1|title=Report: Andre Lima Faces Dong Hun Choi at UFC 327|publisher=pwinsiderxtra.com|author=David Tees|date=2026-01-24|access-date=2026-01-25}}</ref> However, for undisclosed reasons, the bout was moved to [[UFC Fight Night: Burns vs. Malott]], which is scheduled to take place one week later.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmacrossfire.com/2026/02/16/ufc-ottawa-canadian-womens-flyweight-jasmine-jasudavicius-returns-against-karine-silva/|title=UFC Ottawa: Canadian women's flyweight Jasmine Jasudavicius returns against Karine Silva|publisher=mmacrossfire.com|author=Kenai Andrews|date=2026-02-16|access-date=2026-02-18}}</ref>

A middleweight bout between [[Kyle Daukaus]] and [[Vicente Luque]] was scheduled at this event.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://empiresportsmedia.com/mma/ufc-327-adding-vicente-luque-kyle-daukaus/|title=UFC 327 adding Vicente Luque – Kyle Daukaus|publisher=empiresportsmedia.com|author=Daniel Cunningham|date=2026-01-29|access-date=2026-01-30}}</ref> However, Daukaus withdrew after being re‑scheduled for the [[UFC Freedom 250]] event in June and was replaced by former interim [[UFC Middleweight Championship]] challenger (also [[The Ultimate Fighter: Team Jones vs. Team Sonnen]] middleweight winner) [[Kelvin Gastelum]].<ref name="Jiri"/><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/story/sports/ufc/2026/03/13/ufc-white-house-kyle-daukaus-interview-vs-bo-nickal/89121852007/|title=Kyle Daukaus remaining chill despite UFC White House stage, online detractors|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Nolan King|date=2026-03-13|access-date=2026-03-14}}</ref>

A lightweight bout between [[Nazim Sadykhov]] and MarQuel Mederos was scheduled for the event.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.idman.biz/en/news/mma/126375|title=Nazim Sadykhov to face MarQuel Mederos at UFC 327 after cancelled bout two years ago|publisher=idman.biz|author=Idman Biz|date=2026-03-07|access-date=2026-03-07}}</ref> The two were originally expected to meet at [[UFC on ESPN: Namajunas vs. Cortez]] in July 2024 after Mederos stepped in as a replacement fighter, but he withdrew and the bout was scrapped.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2024/06/ufc-denver-fight-card-main-event-lineup-changes|title=UFC Denver fight card loses two more bouts|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Nolan King|date=2024-06-26|access-date=2026-03-07}}</ref> In turn, Sadykhov pulled out in late March due to injury and was replaced by Chris Padilla.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.essentiallysports.com/ufc-mma-news-ufc-327-hit-by-fourth-cancellation-as-lightweight-fighter-withdraws-due-to-injury/|title=UFC 327 hit by fourth cancellation as lightweight fighter withdraws due to injury|publisher=essentiallysports.com|author=Biplob Chakraborty|date=2026-03-23|access-date=2026-03-24}}</ref> At the weigh-ins, Padilla weighed in at 158 pounds, two pounds over the lightweight non-title fight limit. The bout proceeded at catchweight and he was fined 20 percent of his purse, which went to Mederos.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/story/sports/ufc/2026/04/10/ufc-327-jiri-prochazka-vs-carlos-ulberg-light-heavyweight-title-weigh-in-results-live-stream/89533919007/|title=UFC 327 weigh-in results: One fighter misses weight by same overage on two attempts|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Staff|date=2026-04-10|access-date=2026-04-10}}</ref>

[[Beneil Dariush]] and [[Manuel Torres (fighter)|Manuel Torres]] were expected to meet in a lightweight bout.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/story/sports/ufc/2026/02/27/ufc-327-beneil-dariush-vs-manuel-torres-mateusz-gamrot-esteban-ribovics/88906018007/|title=UFC 327 adds two key lightweight bouts|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Nolan King|date=2026-02-27|access-date=2026-02-27}}</ref> However, Torres pulled out due to injury, leaving Dariush without an opponent. As a result, Dariush was removed from the card and was re‑scheduled to compete at [[UFC Fight Night: Della Maddalena vs. Prates]] one month later against [[Quillan Salkilld]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/story/sports/ufc/2026/03/11/ufc-327-beneil-dariush-calls-out-paddy-pimblett-loses-opponent-manuel-torres/89096189007/|title=Beneil Dariush calls out Paddy Pimblett after losing UFC 327 opponent|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Farah Hannoun|date=2026-03-11|access-date=2026-03-11}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://cagesidepress.com/2026/03/12/beneil-dariush-draws-quillan-salkilld-at-ufc-perth-on-may-2/|title=Beneil Dariush draws Quillan Salkilld at UFC Perth on May 2|publisher=cagesidepress.com|author=Eddie Law|date=2026-03-12|access-date=2026-03-13}}</ref>
	
A welterweight bout between Francisco Prado and promotional newcomer Christopher Alvidrez was originally scheduled for this event.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.cbssports.com/ufc/news/ufc-327-fight-card-jiri-prochazka-vs-carlos-ulberg-miami/|title=UFC 327 fight card: Jiri Prochazka vs. Carlos Ulberg set to headline event in Miami for vacant 205-pound title|publisher=cbssports.com|author=Brent Brookhouse|date=2026-02-27|access-date=2026-03-30}}</ref> However, Alvidrez withdrew for unknown reasons and was replaced by Charles Radtke.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Francisco Prado define su destino en Miami: El 'Guerrero de San Lorenzo' regresa en UFC 327|url=https://dia0.com.ar/contenido/5368/francisco-prado-define-su-destino-en-miami-el-guerrero-de-san-lorenzo-regresa-en|date=2026-03-28|access-date=2026-03-30|publisher=dia0.com.ar|author=Día 0}} {{in lang|es}}</ref>

== Bonus awards ==
The following fighters received $100,000 bonuses. The other finishes received $25,000 additional bonuses.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmamania.com/ufc-bonuses-and-awards/435794/official-ufc-327-post-fight-bonuses-results-cbs-paramount-miami-prochazka-ulberg-hokit-blaydes|title=Bonuses! Headstrong heavyweight Josh Hokit double dips at UFC 327, walks away with $200,000|publisher=mmamania.com|author=Dan Hiergesell|date=2026-04-12|access-date=2026-04-12}}</ref>
*'''Fight of the Night: Josh Hokit vs. Curtis Blaydes'''
*'''Performance of the Night: Carlos Ulberg''' and '''Josh Hokit'''
